private_dependency_receipt_mandatory_cla: receipt proves only the declared bounded review result
receipt does not prove full private implementation

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: Stable RFC identity
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement: Every AAOS RFC MUST have one stable RFC identifier that remains distinct from its version, repository path, issue, pull request, commit, tree, rendered document digest, and release identifier.

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: Exact candidate binding
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement:
Every material review disposition and every RFC lifecycle transition MUST bind the exact candidate version and canonical serialized candidate digest.

When materialized in a repository, the disposition MUST also bind repository, commit, and tree identity.

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: Explicit normative sections
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement:
An AAOS RFC MUST identify which sections and requirement records are normative and which content is informative.

Examples, rationale, diagrams, notes, and historical descriptions MUST NOT silently create normative obligations.

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: Normative keyword profile
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement:
AAOS RFCs MUST interpret uppercase normative keywords according to RFC 2119 as clarified by RFC 8174, subject to the AAOS requirement-record rules.

AAOS-authored normative text SHOULD use:

MUST
MUST NOT
SHOULD
SHOULD NOT
MAY

New AAOS normative text SHOULD NOT use `SHALL` or `SHALL NOT`.

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: SHOULD-exception record
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement: An implementation or profile that does not follow a `SHOULD` or `SHOULD NOT` requirement MUST record the justification, affected requirement IDs, risk, compensating behavior, evidence, scope, and validity interval.

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: One primary requirement identifier
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement: Every normative obligation MUST have exactly one primary `AAOS-REQ-<RFC>-<NUMBER>` identifier.

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: Specialized identifier mapping
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement:
Specialized contract, interface, conformance, fixture, test, schema, evidence-class, and record identifiers MUST map to a primary AAOS requirement where they implement or evaluate a normative obligation.

Examples include:

TG-IFACE-*
TG-CONF-*
TG-CONTRACT-EV-*
TG-FIX-*
TG-AP-*
TG-AR-*
TG-TP-*

They MUST NOT become competing primary IDs for the same obligation.

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: Requirement identifier immutability
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement:
A requirement identifier MUST NOT be reused for unrelated or materially incompatible semantics.

Editorial and non-semantic clarification MAY preserve the ID. A breaking semantic replacement MUST create an explicit successor requirement or major RFC revision.

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: Historical resolvability
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement: Deprecated, superseded, withdrawn, and rejected requirement identifiers MUST remain historically resolvable with their prior text, version, status, and successor references.

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: Requirement verification classification
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement:
Every normative requirement MUST declare one or more verification classes:

machine_testable
evidence_reviewable
human_disposition_only
private_l0_dependent

It MUST also declare required evidence, negative behavior, unknown behavior, and public claim boundary.

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: Namespace separation
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement: `rfc_class`, `rfc_review_state`, `rfc_status`, archival designation, delivery state, implementation state, conformance outcome, and deployment state MUST remain separate namespaces.

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: RFC class registry
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement:
RFC-0000 MUST define the following initial RFC classes or explicitly disposition each alternative:

process
standards_track
profile
informational
experimental

RFC class MUST NOT imply lifecycle status, implementation maturity, deployment suitability, or conformance.

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: RFC review-state registry
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement:
RFC-0000 MUST define an RFC review-state namespace that includes at least:

not_started
under_review
changes_requested
ready_for_disposition
blocked_pending_private_l0_input
blocked_by_contract_contradiction

Review state MUST NOT create normative force.

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: RFC lifecycle-status registry
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement:
RFC-0000 MUST define an RFC lifecycle-status namespace that includes at least:

draft
candidate
provisional
accepted
stable
deprecated
superseded
withdrawn
rejected

Each status MUST have a definition, permitted predecessor and successor states, transition authority, required evidence, and migration consequence.

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: Explicit status transitions
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement:
No RFC lifecycle transition MAY be inferred from GitHub state, publication, CI, evaluator output, runtime behavior, implementation availability, conformance test results, or elapsed time.

Every material transition MUST use an exact-candidate-bound human disposition record.

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: Historical projection
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement: `historical` MUST be represented as a derived archival designation or publication projection, not as a competing RFC class and lifecycle status.

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: Review-role separation
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement: RFC-0000 MUST distinguish proposer, editor, semantic owner, required reviewers, private-L0 reviewer where applicable, and final disposer.

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: No self-acceptance by default
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement:
A proposer or editor MUST NOT assign `accepted` or `stable` status solely by virtue of proposing or editing the RFC.

Any permitted role combination MUST be explicitly authorized and its independence limitation MUST be recorded.

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: Disposition record contract
Planning review versus RFC status
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement: A review of mutable issue text MAY assign only planning review state. It MUST NOT assign `candidate`, `provisional`, `accepted`, or `stable` RFC status until an immutable candidate identity exists.

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: Independent revision classification
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement:
Every RFC revision MUST declare both a semantic `revision_class` and a resulting RFC version.

Initial revision classes MUST include:

editorial
clarifying
additive_compatible
restrictive
breaking

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: Semantic versioning baseline
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement:
AAOS RFC versions SHOULD use `MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH`.

Default mapping:

editorial or non-conformance-impact clarification
→ PATCH

additive compatible
→ MINOR

restrictive change that invalidates prior conformant behavior
→ MAJOR unless an accepted transition profile preserves bounded compatibility

breaking
→ MAJOR or successor RFC

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: Clarification cannot hide semantic change
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement: A revision MUST NOT be classified as `clarifying` when it changes required evidence, permitted Authority, object or state meaning, failure behavior, interface obligations, conformance outcomes, or public claim boundaries.

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: Emergency Constraint Notice
ECN cannot widen authority
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement: An Emergency Constraint Notice MUST NOT expand Authority, waive mandatory evidence, convert `not_evaluable` into a permissive result, or become permanent without a normal RFC revision and disposition.

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: Public semantic precedence
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement:
RFC-0000 MUST define public semantic precedence so that lower-level artifacts cannot redefine an accepted public Semantic Core requirement.

Initial precedence candidate:

accepted RFC
→ accepted RFC-owned requirement registry
→ accepted ADR interpretation where delegated by the RFC
→ implementation contract
→ schema, API, or event representation
→ tests, fixtures, and validators
→ runtime behavior
→ informational documentation
→ historical milestone text

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: Private institutional applicability
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement:
Private L0 doctrine MAY determine whether, where, under whose Authority, and with which restrictions an institution adopts a public RFC or profile.

It MUST NOT silently rewrite the public RFC's canonical semantics.

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: Public/private contradiction behavior
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement:
A material contradiction between a public RFC and required private doctrine MUST result in bounded profile restriction, non-adoption, `blocked_pending_private_l0_input`, or `blocked_by_contract_contradiction`.

It MUST NOT use last-write-wins, newest-document-wins, or silent semantic override.

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: No public overclaim
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement: Acceptance of a public RFC MUST NOT be represented as disclosure, implementation evidence, or independent verification of private sovereign controls.

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: Artifact-type separation
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement: Issue, ADR, RFC, requirement registry, schema, interface contract, test, fixture, validator, runtime control, conformance claim, deployment admission, and institutional decision MUST remain distinct artifact types.

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: Downstream traceability
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement: Every schema, interface contract, event contract, validator, test, fixture, runtime control, and conformance claim that implements or evaluates an RFC obligation MUST trace to stable RFC and primary requirement IDs.

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: Test result boundary
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement: Passing a schema validator, evaluator, fixture, test suite, or CI workflow MUST NOT by itself establish live control enforcement, institutional adoption, deployment authorization, certification, or risk acceptance.

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: RFC status boundary
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement: `accepted` or `stable` RFC status MUST NOT authorize implementation, release, deployment, certification, risk acceptance, waiver, audit closure, rollback, restore, cutover, or proof sealing.

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: Unknown mandatory semantics
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement:
An unsupported or unknown mandatory RFC semantic MUST result in `fail_closed`, `not_evaluable`, or explicit human disposition according to the applicable profile.

It MUST NOT be ignored, guessed, downgraded, or mapped to the nearest permissive behavior.

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: Semantic Core intake restriction
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement:
A new Semantic Core RFC or primary issue MUST be justified by an unowned irreducible concept, canonical contradiction, necessary breaking change, authority-boundary or fail-closed defect, or independently reviewable profile after base stabilization.

External novelty alone MUST NOT create a normative RFC.

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: Append-only specification history
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement: Accepted RFC versions, requirement records, dispositions, and conformance claims MUST remain historically preserved after deprecation, supersession, withdrawal, or rejection.

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: Migration-effect declaration
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement: Deprecation, supersession, restrictive revision, breaking revision, ECN issuance, and requirement withdrawal MUST declare effects on implementations, profiles, schemas, tests, deployment admissions, conformance claims, and public claim language.

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: No successor-conformance inference
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement: A conformance claim against one RFC version MUST NOT be represented as conformance to a successor RFC unless the successor requirement set has been evaluated against the exact implementation and environment.

ordered_primary_requirement_records.title: Bounded migration windows
ordered_primary_requirement_records.normative_statement:
A migration window MAY preserve the validity of a historical claim only within its original RFC version, evaluated subject, environment, restrictions, and validity interval.

It MUST NOT imply conformance to the successor RFC.
